title: Polynomial MLP Audit
research_question: If polynomial-style MLPs are live on this backbone, what kind of polynomial behavior does the model actually want?

candidates.name: x1-relu2-control
candidates.goal: Establish the local polynomial-family control run.
candidates.hypothesis: Current relu^2 remains the right baseline inside the polynomial family.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Provides the local reference for all other polynomial variants.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the correct anchor inside a tranche that changes only polynomial structure.
candidates.description: run the current hybrid winner with relu^2 under the new MLP-mode surface

candidates.name: x2-relu3
candidates.hypothesis: A cubic positive path may be more expressive than relu^2, but may also be more fragile.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the frontier wants stronger positive amplification than a square.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest direct escalation from the current relu^2 path.

candidates.name: x3-relu-linear-quad
candidates.goal: Mix gentle and stronger polynomial behavior.
candidates.hypothesis: The model may want both a linear positive path and a quadratic amplification path.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether mixed polynomial behavior beats pure quadratic behavior.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests a true structural alternative, not just a stronger polynomial order.

candidates.name: x4-relu2-cubic
candidates.goal: Test whether a small cubic term improves on pure quadratic behavior.
candidates.hypothesis: A mostly quadratic path with a little cubic emphasis may be the best compromise.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether richer polynomial shaping is helpful without going fully cubic.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most justified richer polynomial variant once pure cubic is represented.
candidates.description: use relu^2 plus a small cubic term

candidates.name: x5-norm-square
candidates.goal: Test whether polynomial behavior works better when stabilized first.
candidates.hypothesis: A normalized square path may keep the benefits of polynomial shaping while being more quantization- and optimization-friendly.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the main issue in polynomial MLPs is the nonlinearity or its pre-activation scale.
candidates.why_worthy: This turns the polynomial tranche into a true mechanism study rather than only an order sweep.
candidates.description: apply RMSNorm before squaring in the MLP hidden path
